data.title: Ameriprise Financial upgraded at Piper Sandler after Q4 earnings
data.description:
Ameriprise Financial (AMP) was upgraded at Piper Sandler on the back of stability in the margins of the financial services provider's largest business – Advice & Wealth Management.

The stock was raised to Neutral from Underweight, and the price target was increased to $530.00 from $434.00. Shares were +1.24% to $527.82 on Friday after the opening bell.

The Minneapolis-based company reported Q4 non-GAAP EPS of $10.83, which beats by $0.52, and revenue of $5.05B (+8.6% Y/Y), which beats by $310M.

"Many headwinds present since 3Q24 leading to share price underperformance on a relative basis appear to be somewhat receding," said analyst John Barnidge.

"Results demonstrated the moat built is underappreciated by both us and investors," said the research note.

Piper Sandler's rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating of Hold. On the other hand, SA authors grade AMP as Buy.

data.title: Chicago PMI rises far more than expected in January
data.description: The Chicago PMI surged to 54.0 in January from 42.7 in the prior month, which was revised from 43.5, exceeding the 43.5 consensus, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Friday.

data.title: Red River Bancshares GAAP EPS of $1.73, revenue of $33.19M
data.description:
• Red River Bancshares press release (RRBI): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.73.
  • Revenue of $33.19M.
  • As of December 31, 2025, loans held for investment (“HFI”) were $2.25 billion, up $75.6 million, or 3.5%, from $2.17 billion as of September 30, 2025. In the fourth quarter of 2025, we experienced robust new loan and commitment activity, combined with funding of loan construction commitments.
  • As of December 31, 2025, assets were $3.35 billion, up $136.5 million, or 4.2%, from $3.21 billion as of September 30, 2025, driven by a $124.6 million increase in deposits.
  • Deposits totaled $2.96 billion as of December 31, 2025, up $124.6 million, or 4.4%, from $2.84 billion as of September 30, 2025, primarily due to the seasonal inflow of funds from public entity customers combined with higher customer deposit balances.
  • We paid a quarterly cash dividend of $0.15 per common share in the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • In 2025, our cash dividend was $0.54 per common share, which was a 50.0% increase from $0.36 per common share paid in 2024.
  • The 2025 stock repurchase program authorized us to purchase up to $5.0 million of our outstanding shares of common stock from January 1, 2025 through December 31, 2025. We repurchased shares on the open market during the second quarter of 2025, when we repurchased 11,748 shares at an aggregate cost of $656,000, excluding excise tax. The 2025 stock repurchase program expired on December 31, 2025, with $4.3 million of available capacity.
  •

data.title: Flagstar Bank returns to profit in Q4 after two-year loss streak
data.description:
Flagstar Bank (FLG) on Friday delivered its first profitable quarter in two years as stronger net interest income, lower expenses and easing credit costs helped to lift results. What's more, it issued a better-than-expected adjusted earnings outlook for the year, following an unprofitable 2025.

"In addition to returning to profitability during the fourth quarter, we also had positive operating leverage, which we expect to continue in 2026," Chairman, President and CEO Joseph M. Otting said in a statement. "Our return to profitability during the fourth quarter is a significant milestone in the Bank's turnaround, but it is only one of several positive trends, including continued growth in the [commercial and industrial] portfolio, [net interest margin] expansion coupled with an increase in net interest income, ongoing expense management, and an improving credit quality profile."

For full-year 2026, the company sees adjusted EPS of $0.65-$0.70 (midpoint $0.675), topping the $0.61 Wall Street consensus; NII of $2.15B-$2.20B (midpoint $2.175B), compared with the $2.17B Visible Alpha estimate; NIM of 2.40%-2.60% (midpoint 2.50%), vs. 2.44% Visible Alpha estimate; provision for loan losses of $200M-$250M (midpoint $225M), vs. $209M Visible Alpha estimate; noninterest income of $325M-$365M (midpoint $345M); and adjusted operating expense of $1.75B-$1.80B (midpoint $1.775B), according to a slide presentation.

Meanwhile, Q4 adjusted EPS of $0.06, topping the $0.02 average analyst estimate, rose from -$0.07 in Q3 and -$0.40 a year earlier.

Q4 total revenue of $557M, vs. $531.5M expected, advanced from $519M in the prior quarter but fell from $625M in last year's Q4.

FLG shares were little changed minutes after the opening bell

Net interest income of $467M, vs. $454.2M Visible Alpha estimate, increased 10% Q/Q and 1% Y/Y.

Total adjusted noninterest income was $81M in Q4, up from $73M in Q3 and $72M in Q4 2024.

Provision for credit losses dropped to $3M from $38M in Q3 and $145M in Q4 2024.

Adjusted operating expenses of $462M edged up 1% sequentially and retreated 17% from a year earlier.

Total loans and leases held for investment were $60.7B at Dec. 31, 2025, down 3% Q/Q. Deposits totaled $66.0B at the end of Q4, down 5% Q/Q.

data.title: Republic Bancorp Non-GAAP EPS of $1.17
data.description:
• Republic Bancorp press release (RBCAA): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.17.

  • The net charge of $367,000 for the fourth quarter of 2024 was driven, primarily, by the following:

    • The Core Bank recorded a charge to the Provision of $277,000 related to net charge-offs on loans.
    • Consistent with a nominal increase in Traditional Bank loan balances, a net charge of $270,000 was recorded for the fourth quarter of 2024.
    • Outstanding Warehouse balances declined $44 million during the quarter, driving a $112,000 net credit to the Provision.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 slide as tech-led selloff looks to deepen; Trump picks new Fed chair
data.description:
The stock market opened lower on Friday as the selloff looked to deepen as investors digested more earnings and Donald Trump's Fed chair selection. 

S&P 500 (SP500) -0.3%, the Dow (DJI) -0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.5%.

Apple (AAPL) released its first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results that were driven by record-breaking iPhone sales.

Visa's (V) fiscal Q1 2026 earnings and revenue topped the average analyst estimates. 

The producer price index came in hotter than expected in December.

Trump said he has selected Kevin Warsh to be the next Federal Reserve chair. 

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3.2 basis points to 4.27%, and the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) advanced 4.6 basis points to 4.90%. The shorter-end 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) edged higher to 3.57%.

data.title: Precious metals reverse hard, and these gold and silver ETFs sink after an explosive run
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and silver (XAGUSD:CUR) mining and spot-based exchange traded funds are under notable pressure Friday morning as precious metals retraced sharply following a powerful recent rally.

Gold prices are down roughly 6%, and are looking to stabilize just above $5,000 per ounce after briefly surging past $5,500 earlier in the week. Silver has experienced an even steeper pullback, sliding approximately 15% to fall back below $100 per ounce after spiking above $120 only days ago. 

The swift reversal has shifted market attention toward vehicles tracking the metals directly, as well as companies exposed to their production. Spot gold and silver ETFs have moved decisively lower, while mining-focused funds are also posting substantial losses as trading begins. 

The selloff reflects a combination of profit-taking after outsized gains and heightened short-term volatility across commodity markets. Despite the abrupt move, prices for both metals remain well above levels seen earlier this year, underscoring the magnitude of the prior advance. 

Investors are now assessing whether the decline represents a healthy consolidation within a broader bullish trend or the early stages of a more sustained correction. 

Gold and Gold Mining ETFs: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), and (DUST). 

Silver and Silver Mining ETFs: (SLV), (SIVR), (AGQ), (ZSL), (PSLV), (SIL), (SILJ), and (SLVP).

data.title: Corcept down as FDA revises rejection letter for hormonal disorder therapy
data.description:
• Corcept Therapeutics (CORT) lost ~16% in the premarket on Friday after the FDA revised a Complete Response Letter issued last month in connection with the company’s marketing application for its hormonal disorder therapy relacorilant.
  • The agency declined to approve the New Drug Application filed by the Redwood City, California-based pharma, noting it could not determine a favorable benefit-risk assessment for the drug without additional data, according to a company press release in December.
  • The NDA was aimed at seeking U.S. approval of relacorilant for patients with high blood pressure related to a hormonal disorder called hypercortisolism (Cushing's syndrome).
  • In the partially redacted letter dated Jan. 28, the FDA noted that while the prior CRL contained no errors, it “is being updated to respond to Corcept’s communication.”
